task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new field named 'ASP' in the data elements panel or a calculation editor window showing the creation of 'ASP'.
Look for a bar chart rendered on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Set the X-axis to Product Sub Category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Product Sub Category' assigned to the Category (X-axis) in the visualization grammar panel, or as the labels on the chart's X-axis.

task_completion.description: Set the Y-axis to the custom ASP calculation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ASP' assigned to the Values (Y-axis) in the visualization grammar panel, or as the label on the chart's Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Color the bars by Product Category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Product Category' assigned to the Color section in the visualization grammar panel, and verify the chart has a legend for Product Category with correspondingly colored bars.

task_completion.description: Sort the chart by ASP in descending order
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bar chart to ensure the bars are ordered from tallest (highest ASP) on the left to shortest (lowest ASP) on the right.

task_completion.description: Save the workbook as 'ASP_Analysis' to the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the application title bar displaying 'ASP_Analysis' or a save dialog showing the file being saved as 'ASP_Analysis.dva' in the C:\Users\Docker\Documents directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual analytics application GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface being used to build the chart, rather than a terminal window, Python script, or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a simple average function for the calculation
integrity.visual_evidence: If the calculation editor is visible, ensure the formula uses a division of sums (e.g., SUM(Sales)/SUM(Quantity Ordered)) and not a simple AVG() function.

integrity.description: The file was saved via the application's save functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the application's native save dialog or the updated title bar in the GUI, rather than terminal commands like 'touch' or 'echo' being used to create a dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is 'ASP_Analysis.dva' located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'. The visualization must be a Bar chart with 'Product Sub Category' on the X-axis, 'ASP' on the Y-axis, and colored by 'Product Category'. Note: The dataset actually contains 'Sales' and 'Quantity Ordered' columns, so the ASP calculation must be based on these columns (e.g., SUM(Sales) / SUM(Quantity Ordered)) despite the prompt mentioning 'Revenue' and 'Quantity'. Simple averages (like AVG(Sales)) are forbidden.
